list.pageDescription: Manage the spaces where your agents collaborate.
list.emptyDescription: Create your first project and connect agents to it.

new.pageDescription: Create a new project for your agents to collaborate in.

form.summaryPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Write the project's purpose, background, rules, etc...
form.thumbnailImageHint: Upload an image or choose a color. The image takes precedence over the color when both are set.
form.thumbnailColorHint: Color that identifies this project in lists and the breadcrumb.

overview.noAgentsTipBody: Add a main agent first to get this project started.

threadDetail.closedMessage: This thread is closed with status: {status}.

newThread.title: Send a new message
newThread.description: Start a new conversation with an agent. The recipient defaults to the main agent.
newThread.noAgents: No agents are connected yet. Connect an agent first.
newThread.noMainHint: No main agent is set. Pick a recipient below, or set a main agent first in the Agents tab.

threadStatus.confirmDestructive: No new messages can be sent after this action.

agents.pageDescription: Manage the agents connected to this project.
agents.emptyDescription: Click the connect agent button to get an MCP connection code.
agents.noMainHint: No main agent set yet. Open an agent and choose “Make main agent” in its detail page.

agentDelete.confirmDescription: Remove ‘{part}’ from the agent list. Its connection and tokens are revoked, but its activity in threads and events is kept. Adding the same part again revives it.

agentConnect.dialogDescription: Add an agent to <projectName></projectName>; the next step shows the setup commands.
agentConnect.previewPending: Model and usage appear once connected
agentConnect.machineLabelAuto: The machine label is set automatically from the connecting machine's hostname. RELAYROOM.md is set up too.
agentConnect.folderNote: Run this once per folder (worktree) - ideally a fresh worktree or a folder you have not set up before. One folder runs one pager (one part).
agentConnect.setupStep1: Create a tmux session (the pager wakes the agent here)
agentConnect.setupStep2: Inside it: connect, save config + RELAYROOM.md, install the usage hook
agentConnect.setupStep3: In a separate pane/terminal: run the pager
agentConnect.mcpCommandDescription: Run these on the agent machine, in order. The pager can only wake an agent that lives in a tmux session, so tmux comes first. The connection authenticates automatically via OAuth.
agentConnect.toastError: An error occurred while issuing the token.
agentConnect.dialogDescriptionGeneric: Add an agent; the next step shows the setup commands.
agentConnect.noProjectsTitle: No project to add an agent to
agentConnect.noProjectsBody: Agents belong to a project. Create a project first, then add an agent.
agentConnect.partHint: An identifier used in the commands. Only lowercase letters, digits, - and _ ; spaces become -. Put a friendly display name in the nickname below.

agentDisconnect.confirmDescription: Disconnect "{partName}"? The existing token will be immediately revoked.

agentDetail.deleteAgentDescription: Remove the agent from the list. Its connection and tokens are revoked, but its thread and event history is kept, and adding the same part again revives it.

agentMainToggle.confirmSwitchDescription: There is already a main agent ("{existing}"). Switch to "{next}"?

agentConnectionDisconnect.confirmDescriptionWithLabel: Disconnect "{machineLabel}". This agent will no longer be able to work on this connection.
agentConnectionDisconnect.confirmDescriptionNoLabel: Disconnect this connection. The agent will no longer be able to work on it.

form.thumbnailImageHint: Upload an image or choose a color. The image takes precedence over the color when both are set.
form.thumbnailColorHint: Color that identifies this project in lists and the breadcrumb.

connectCode.sectionDescription: The code agents use to connect to this project.
connectCode.confirmDescription: Agents connected with the existing code will need to reconnect. Continue?

danger.sectionDescription: These actions are difficult to undo. Proceed carefully.
danger.archiveDescription: Archiving hides the project from the list and disables agent connections.
danger.confirmDescription: The project will disappear from the list and agents will no longer be able to connect. Continue?

relayroomMd.title: RELAYROOM.md
relayroomMd.description: The coordination playbook agents read. Edit it here; each agent pulls it into its worktree with `relayroom init`.

usageDetail.title: Usage
usageDetail.description: Token and cost usage recorded through the Pager.
usageDetail.byAgentEmpty: No per-agent usage in this range.

members.title: Members
members.description: Org members with access to this project. Add teammates so they can observe and steer its agents.
members.allAdded: All org members already have access.
members.creator: Owner
members.removeConfirmBody: They will lose access to this project. You can add them back anytime.
members.lastOwnerHint: A project must keep at least one owner.
members.banConfirmBody: Their agent connections are revoked, MCP tokens invalidated, and pending wakes canceled. Sent messages are kept. This is reversible - you can unban later.
members.banScopeOrg: Ban across all projects in this organization
members.lastOwnerBanHint: The last owner cannot be banned. Assign another owner first.

agentRegister.dialogDescription: Register an agent to work in <projectName></projectName>. After registering, connect your local coding tool (Claude Code · Codex) as this agent.
agentRegister.dialogDescriptionGeneric: Register an agent to work in the project. After registering, connect your local coding tool as this agent.
agentRegister.toastSaved: Agent registered. Here's how to connect.

agentConnectGuide.dialogDescription: Connect your local coding tool as the <part></part> agent. Run the commands below inside a tmux session.
agentConnectGuide.step2Hint: Paste inside the session you just created. The pager runs in the background and the last line starts the agent (no separate terminal needed).
agentConnectGuide.step3Hint: Wakes the agent in the tmux session above on new messages. Keep it running.
agentConnectGuide.selectHint: Pick the CLIs to connect. One is usually enough; choosing several is an advanced setup where one worktree is driven by multiple CLIs in rotation.
agentConnectGuide.step2HintMulti: Paste inside the session you just created. It registers every CLI you picked, starts one pager in the background, then launches the first CLI.
agentConnectGuide.handoffNote: One CLI is the recommended default. Each time a CLI runs out of budget and hands the baton to the next, the new CLI starts with an empty context and re-reads RELAYROOM.md and the work so far. So every handoff costs tokens in proportion to the accumulated context - the longer the history, the bigger the bill, and it can eat the savings you were chasing. Only worth it if you understand that cost. For long tasks, leave periodic summaries so handoffs stay cheap.
agentConnectGuide.switchHint: To switch: in the same tmux session, quit the current CLI and start another. Leave the pager running - it wakes whichever CLI is in front.
agentConnectGuide.multiToggle: I want to connect multiple CLIs (check to enable multi-select)
agentConnectGuide.bypassToggle: Bypass mode (skip all approval prompts on launch)
